message:
test(config): form-feed (\x0c) handling in split_config_line_args

Adds assertions matching the \x0b vertical-tab coverage to cover
\x0c form feed (the other classic Unix "whitespace-ish but not
ASCII space/tab/LF" character) so both sides of the
sdssplitargs-equivalent tokeniser stay consistent.

crates/fr-config/src/lib.rs:

- New unit test redis_config_line_split_preserves_form_feed_in_
  bare_tokens asserts that \x0c inside a bare token is retained
  (so "foo\x0cbar baz" -> ["foo\x0cbar", "baz"]) and that \x0c
  immediately after a closed double-quoted token is treated as
  a whitespace separator ("\"foo\"\x0cbar" -> ["foo", "bar"]).

crates/fr-conformance/tests/smoke.rs:

- redis_config_argument_splitter_conformance_matrix grows the
  same two assertions so the conformance suite pins the
  behaviour visible to downstream callers, not just the unit
  tests in fr-config.

.beads/issues.jsonl:

- br sync --flush-only for the follow-up bead. Status-only
  transitions.

 {"id":"frankenredis-4ai0","title":"Add conformance fixtures validating all stub remediations","description":"# Add conformance fixtures for all stub remediations

## What
After each stub is replaced with a real implementation, add conformance test cases that would have FAILED with the old stub but PASS with the new implementation. This proves the fix works end-to-end.

## Fixtures to add/update

### core_server.json (INFO section)
- INFO stats after SET/GET operations should show non-zero keyspace_hits/misses
- INFO stats after key expiry should show non-zero expired_keys
- INFO clients should show correct blocked_clients count

### core_server.json (LATENCY section)
- CONFIG SET latency-monitor-threshold 1 → run DEBUG SLEEP 0.01 → LATENCY LATEST should return samples
- LATENCY HISTORY command should return entries
- LATENCY RESET should clear and return count

### core_server.json (SLOWLOG via Lua)
- EVAL \"return redis.call('SLOWLOG','LEN')\" should return real count (not 0)

### core_server.json (DEBUG section)
- DEBUG OBJECT <key> should return encoding info string
- DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0 should return OK

### core_scripting.json (Lua CLIENT)
- redis.call(\"CLIENT\", \"ID\") should return integer > 0 (not always 1)

### core_copy.json or new fixture
- ACL SAVE + ACL LOAD round-trip test

### core_stream.json
- XADD + XDEL + XINFO STREAM should show correct max-deleted-entry-id

## Depends on
- All implementation beads (this is the verification layer)

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_server.json
- crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_scripting.json
- crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_stream.json
- crates/fr-conformance/tests/smoke.rs (if new fixtures added)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:08.752602017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:16:39.064325316Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:16:39.064068490Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-4ai0","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1qdg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:35.095210715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-4ai0","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-2auy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:35.463212327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-4ai0","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-7ews","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:34.724984593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-4ai0","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-djx8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:35.338495229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-4ai0","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-egag","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:35.218914344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-4ai0","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kzxw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:34.847390346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-4ai0","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-nwgw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:34.969929100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-4ai0","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-xvvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:34.600855870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-57ga","title":"EPIC: Align README and FEATURE_PARITY claims with actual implementation reality","description":"# Documentation Honesty Epic

## Why
The reality check identified three areas where project self-assessment is inflated:

1. FEATURE_PARITY.md claims "parity_green" for replication — false (skeleton only)
2. README describes "Methodological DNA" items (RaptorQ, alien-artifact-coding, extreme-optimization) as current project characteristics — they are aspirational roadmap items
3. README claims "profile-proven performance" — no workload benchmarks exist

Honest documentation is a prerequisite for good engineering. Inflated claims waste other agents time and create false confidence.

## What Success Looks Like
- FEATURE_PARITY.md statuses match reality
- README clearly distinguishes implemented features from roadmap items
- "Current State" section accurately describes what works TODAY
- Aspirational methodology items are in a clearly labeled "Roadmap" section","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:45.320908329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:11:42.925617169Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:11:42.925325767Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: 8xmq (replication status downgrade), fw60 (FEATURE_PARITY review + benchmark status downgrade), gmmz (README methodology/roadmap split). All claims now verifiable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-57ga","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-8xmq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.651483099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-57ga","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-fw60","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.511480127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-57ga","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-gmmz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.369741030Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -137,7 +138,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-be8t.5","title":"Evaluate jemalloc/mimalloc as global allocator for fr-server","description":"# Evaluate Alternative Global Allocators

## What

The April 9 flamegraph shows malloc (3.13%) + cfree (5.70%) + _int_malloc
(2.42%) = ~11% of CPU spent in the glibc allocator. Redis uses jemalloc by
default, which is optimized for the many-small-allocation pattern of
key-value stores.

## Why

glibc malloc is a general-purpose allocator. For workloads with many small,
short-lived allocations (RESP frame parsing, response serialization, key
copies), specialized allocators can provide:
- Lower fragmentation
- Better cache locality (thread-local arenas)
- Faster small-allocation paths (slab allocators)
- Reduced lock contention (though FrankenRedis is single-threaded, allocator
  internal locks still exist in glibc)

## Candidates

### jemalloc (via tikv-jemallocator crate)
- Redis's default allocator
- Excellent for mixed-size workloads
- Well-tested in production Redis deployments
- ~200KB binary size increase

### mimalloc (via mimalloc crate)
- Microsoft's allocator, optimized for modern workloads
- Often faster than jemalloc for small allocations
- Lower memory overhead
- ~100KB binary size increase

## Implementation

Add to crates/fr-server/Cargo.toml:
```toml
[dependencies]
tikv-jemallocator = { version = "0.6", optional = true }
```

In main.rs:
```rust
#[cfg(feature = "jemalloc")]
#[global_allocator]
static GLOBAL: tikv_jemallocator::Jemalloc = tikv_jemallocator::Jemalloc;
```

## Verification

Benchmark with and without the feature flag:
1. fr-bench all workloads (set/get/incr/mixed, p1 and p16)
2. Flamegraph comparison — allocator functions should drop from ~11% to <5%
3. Memory usage comparison under sustained load (100K keys, 1M operations)
4. No unsafe code introduction — the allocator crate handles this

## Note on #![forbid(unsafe_code)]

The global allocator crates use unsafe internally but present a safe API.
The #[global_allocator] attribute is safe Rust. The fr-server binary can
use this without violating the unsafe_code forbid on the library crates.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Benchmark comparison (jemalloc vs glibc) for all workloads
2. If improvement >5%, add jemalloc as default feature for fr-server
3. If improvement <5%, close as "not worth the dependency"
4. Memory usage not significantly increased (< 10% overhead)
5. All tests pass with the new allocator","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:51.149254321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T17:42:54.521734457Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T17:42:54.521456861Z","close_reason":"Completed evaluation. Allocators are wired up correctly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","perf","server"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.5","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-be8t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:51.149254321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-be8t.6","title":"Investigate redundant drop_if_expired/exists calls per command path","description":"# Investigate Redundant Expiry Checks Per Command

## What

In the April 9 flamegraph, Store::drop_if_expired is 4.45% and Store::exists
is 4.36% of CPU. These functions perform key lookups with expiry checks. The
question is whether multiple expiry checks fire for the same key within a
single command path.

## Hypothesis

A typical SET command path may traverse:
1. execute_frame_internal → dispatch
2. dispatch_argv → set handler
3. set handler calls self.entries.get/get_mut (which might call drop_if_expired)
4. set handler calls exists/drop_if_expired explicitly before insert
5. If SETEX/PSETEX: additional expire path

If drop_if_expired is called 2-3 times for the same key in the same command,
the second and third calls are wasted — the key can't re-expire between calls
within a synchronous single-threaded execution.

## Investigation Steps

1. Trace all call sites of drop_if_expired in fr-store/src/lib.rs
2. For each Store method (set_string, get_string, exists, etc.), count how
   many times drop_if_expired is called per logical operation
3. Identify redundant calls where the same key is checked multiple times
4. Propose consolidation: either check once at entry point, or use a
   "just checked" sentinel

## Design for Fix (if redundancy confirmed)

### Option A: Check-once wrapper
Each Store method that touches a key calls drop_if_expired once at the top
and marks the entry as "checked this tick." Subsequent calls within the same
now_ms timestamp skip the check.

### Option B: Caller-side dedup
Move the drop_if_expired call to the dispatch layer (execute_db_scoped_command)
and remove it from individual Store methods. The dispatcher pre-expires all
keys the command will touch, then the Store methods can assume keys are live.

### Option C: Lazy-only
Don't proactively expire at all in the per-command path. Only expire when a
key is accessed and found to be expired. This is Redis's actual behavior
(lazy expiry + periodic background expiry). The per-access check is just
"if expired, delete and return nil."

Recommended: Option C if conformance allows — it matches Redis most closely.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Investigation report identifying all redundant expiry check paths
2. If redundancy confirmed: fix reduces drop_if_expired CPU share
3. All expiry conformance tests still pass
4. TTL/PTTL commands still return correct values","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:51.258704240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:49:27.509468176Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T23:49:27.509202824Z","close_reason":"Reduced redundant drop_if_expired lookups in SET/EXPIRETIME/XADD/LPOP/RPOP paths","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["expire","perf","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.6","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-be8t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:51.258704240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","title":"Phase 2 comprehensive re-baseline: flamegraph + benchmark + delta artifact after all optimizations","description":"# Phase 2 Comprehensive Re-Baseline

## What

After all Phase 2 optimization subtasks are complete (or a meaningful subset),
run a comprehensive benchmark suite + flamegraph capture and produce a final
delta artifact comparing Phase 2 results to both:
- The original April 7 baseline (frankenredis-zjii era: ~1200 ops/sec)
- The April 9 Phase 1 recovery baseline (~75K-90K ops/sec per-command)
- The Redis 7.2.4 reference numbers

## Steps

1. Clean build: cargo build --release -p fr-server -p fr-bench
2. Start server: ./target/release/frankenredis --port 6399 --mode strict
3. Run fr-bench for ALL workloads at pipeline=1 and pipeline=16:
   set, get, incr, lpush, lpop, hset, hget, mixed
4. Capture perf flamegraph (-e cpu-clock -F 999) under pipeline=16 load
5. Capture strace -c under pipeline=16 load (verify syscall reduction)
6. Compare numbers to all previous baselines
7. Write artifacts/optimization/phase2-final/DELTA_REPORT.md with:
   - Before/after table for all workloads
   - Flamegraph comparison (what changed, what's left)
   - Syscall count comparison
   - Per-optimization-lever attribution (which commit contributed what)
8. Update README.md benchmark section
9. Update baselines/ with new checked-in JSON artifacts

## Target Numbers

Based on the optimization opportunities identified:
- SET p1: >85K ops/sec (from 75K) — HashMap alone should get this
- GET p1: >95K ops/sec (from 90K) — should match or exceed Redis
- SET p16: >400K ops/sec (from 268K) — write coalescing is the lever
- GET p16: >600K ops/sec (from 434K) — same lever

## This Is the Final Gate

This bead should only be claimed after the high-impact subtasks are done.
It produces the evidence that justifies closing the epic.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Complete benchmark table for all workloads at p1 and p16
2. Flamegraph showing no single function >10% (healthy distribution)
3. Delta report artifact checked in
4. README updated with final numbers
5. baselines/ updated with new JSON artifacts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:13.881379570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T17:31:15.231586128Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T17:31:15.231392612Z","close_reason":"Phase 2 final benchmark completed: SET p1=69.6K (74% Redis), GET p1=75.5K (83% Redis), SET p16=284K, GET p16=450K. Delta report written to artifacts/optimization/phase2-final/DELTA_REPORT.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","gate","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-be8t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:13.881379570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-be8t.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:28.253143216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-be8t.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:28.132216622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:28.011644529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-bfp","title":"Wire CONFIG SET maxmemory-samples to eviction subsystem","description":"## Problem

fr-runtime/src/lib.rs lines 961-962:
- maxmemory_eviction_sample_limit: 16 (Redis default is 5, called maxmemory-samples)
- maxmemory_eviction_max_cycles: 4

These are initialized in ServerState::default but neither is wired to CONFIG SET maxmemory-samples. Our default (16) is also higher than Redis's default (5), which affects eviction behavior fidelity.

## What Redis Does

CONFIG SET maxmemory-samples <count> changes the number of keys sampled during each eviction cycle. Higher values give better eviction accuracy at the cost of more CPU.

## Implementation Plan

1. Change default from 16 to 5 (match Redis)
2. Handle CONFIG SET maxmemory-samples → update maxmemory_eviction_sample_limit
3. CONFIG GET maxmemory-samples → return current value

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: Fix default, add CONFIG SET handler","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:16.369290124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:45:05.445385859Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:45:05.444890462Z","close_reason":"maxmemory-samples: default fixed to 5 (was 16), CONFIG SET/GET wired to runtime","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-bfp","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:20.028538611Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
